task: Summarize a synthetic project status note in 2-3 sentences.
input: Project Aurora update (Week 12): The backend API migration to v3 endpoints is 80% complete. Three blockers remain: the legacy auth module has not been refactored, staging environment certificates expire on Friday, and QA sign-off for the payments service is pending. The frontend team is blocked on the API work and has pivoted to accessibility improvements in the interim. No deployment to production is scheduled until all blockers are resolved.
expected_behavior: Produces a 2-3 sentence summary that names the migration progress (80%), the three blockers, and the frontend pivot. Does not introduce facts not present in the source.
must_not_do: Invent blockers not mentioned in the source

task: Summarize a synthetic research abstract in one paragraph.
input: Abstract: This study examines the effect of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) on factual accuracy in long-form question answering. We evaluated three retrieval strategies (dense, sparse, hybrid) across 2,400 synthetic question-answer pairs drawn from a controlled knowledge base. Results show hybrid retrieval achieves 91.4% factual accuracy versus 83.2% for dense-only and 79.8% for sparse-only. Latency overhead for hybrid retrieval averaged 340 ms per query on our test hardware. We conclude that hybrid retrieval meaningfully improves accuracy at an acceptable latency cost for most production workloads.
expected_behavior: One-paragraph summary covering: topic (RAG + factual accuracy), three strategies tested, key result (hybrid best at 91.4%), and latency trade-off. Numbers are preserved accurately.
must_not_do: Add external claims about RAG not in the abstract

task: Summarize a synthetic meeting transcript excerpt into three bullet points.
input:
Transcript excerpt:
Sarah: We need to finalize the data retention policy before the audit in Q3. Legal says we must delete personal data after 90 days unless the user has an active contract.
Marcus: I can implement the deletion job by end of sprint, but I need sign-off from the data team first.
Sarah: Let me reach out to Priya today. Also, we should update the privacy notice on the website.
Marcus: Agreed. I will open a ticket for the notice update.
Sarah: One more thing: the audit checklist from compliance is due next Monday. Marcus, can you fill in the technical sections?
Marcus: Yes, I will have that done by Friday.
expected_behavior: Produces exactly three bullet points covering: (1) data retention policy decision (90 days, delete unless active contract), (2) Marcus implementing deletion job pending sign-off, (3) audit checklist due Monday with Marcus completing technical sections by Friday.
must_not_do: Attribute statements to the wrong person

task: Produce an executive summary (max 50 words) of a synthetic incident report.
input:
Incident Report IR-2024-0441
Date: 2024-03-15  Time: 02:17 UTC  Duration: 47 minutes
Service: Synthetic Payment Gateway  Severity: P1
Summary: A misconfigured rate-limit rule deployed at 02:00 UTC caused 100% of payment authorization requests to return HTTP 429 errors for 47 minutes. 12,300 transactions were dropped. Root cause: a deployment script applied a limit of 0 req/min instead of 1000 req/min. Remediation: rate-limit config was corrected at 02:47 UTC; all services restored. Post-mortem: configuration validation step to be added to CI pipeline.
expected_behavior: Produces an executive summary at or under 50 words that includes: P1 incident, 47-minute outage, 12,300 dropped transactions, root cause (misconfigured rate limit), and remediation (config corrected). Does not add details not in the report.

task: Summarize a synthetic customer feedback batch in 2-4 sentences, noting sentiment distribution.
input:
Feedback batch (5 items):
1. 'The onboarding flow was confusing and I almost gave up.' (negative)
2. 'Excellent support team, resolved my issue in under 10 minutes.' (positive)
3. 'The dashboard is clean but the export feature is broken.' (mixed)
4. 'Best tool I have used for pipeline management, highly recommend.' (positive)
5. 'Took three attempts to reset my password - very frustrating.' (negative)
expected_behavior: 2-4 sentence summary that correctly states 2 positive, 2 negative, 1 mixed. Highlights onboarding/password friction and support speed as key themes. Does not claim all feedback is negative or positive.

task: Summarize a synthetic changelog into a user-facing release note (max 80 words).
input:
Changelog v2.5.0:
- Added: Batch export of up to 500 records to CSV
- Added: Dark mode support across all dashboard views
- Fixed: Memory leak in the background sync worker (GH-1821)
- Fixed: Incorrect date formatting in the Japanese locale (GH-1887)
- Changed: Rate limit on public API raised from 100 to 250 requests per minute
- Deprecated: Legacy XML export endpoint; to be removed in v3.0
expected_behavior: User-facing release note at or under 80 words. Covers two new features (batch export, dark mode), two bug fixes, rate limit increase, and deprecation notice. Uses plain language, not internal ticket numbers.
must_not_do: Include GH issue numbers in the user-facing text

task: Summarize a synthetic legal clause in plain English (max 60 words).
input: Clause 9.3 Limitation of Liability: In no event shall either party be liable to the other for any indirect, incidental, consequential, special, exemplary, or punitive damages arising out of or related to this Agreement, even if such party has been advised of the possibility of such damages. Each party's total cumulative liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid in the preceding twelve-month period.
expected_behavior: Plain-English summary of 60 words or fewer explaining: (1) neither party can claim indirect/consequential damages, (2) total liability capped at prior 12 months of fees. No legal advice offered.

task: Summarize a synthetic financial snapshot in 2-3 sentences.
input:
Synthetic Corp Q2 Financial Snapshot:
Revenue: $4.2M (up 18% YoY)
Gross Margin: 61% (up 3pp YoY)
Operating Expenses: $2.8M (up 22% YoY)
EBITDA: $0.7M (down 11% YoY)
Cash on Hand: $3.1M
Net headcount added: 14
Context: OpEx increase driven by new hires and expanded office lease.
expected_behavior: 2-3 sentences covering: 18% revenue growth, 61% gross margin, EBITDA decline (-11%) driven by OpEx growth from hiring and office expansion, and $3.1M cash position.

task: Condense a synthetic multi-paragraph product spec into a one-sentence TL;DR.
input:
Product Spec: SmartFilter Pro v2
SmartFilter Pro v2 is an automated data cleaning library for Python. It ingests pandas DataFrames and applies a configurable pipeline of heuristic and ML-based rules to detect nulls, outliers, type mismatches, and duplicate rows. Users define rules via a YAML config file. The library integrates with popular ML frameworks including scikit-learn and XGBoost. Processing speed benchmarks show 1M rows cleaned in under 4 seconds on a standard laptop. The library is released under the MIT license and supports Python 3.9+.
expected_behavior: Single sentence that captures: automated Python data cleaning library for DataFrames, configurable via YAML, MIT licensed. Does not need to list every feature.
must_not_do: Omit that it is a Python library

task: Summarize a synthetic support ticket thread, identifying resolution and owner.
input:
Ticket #SYN-9921
User (Day 1): I cannot log in. Getting 'Invalid credentials' even after password reset.
Support Agent (Day 1): Have you cleared your browser cache? Also try incognito.
User (Day 2): Still broken in incognito. Works on mobile app though.
Support Agent (Day 2): Escalating to Tier 2. Likely a web-session cookie issue.
Tier 2 Engineer (Day 3): Found root cause: CDN was caching stale auth tokens. Deployed a cache-invalidation fix. User confirmed login works.
Status: Resolved.
expected_behavior: Summary names: issue (login failure on web), resolution (CDN caching stale auth tokens, fixed with cache invalidation), resolved in 3 days, escalated to Tier 2. Does not confuse mobile and web contexts.
must_not_do: Say the issue was a user error
Misattribute the fix to the original support agent

task: Summarize a synthetic onboarding checklist status email in 3 bullet points.
input:
Hi team,
Quick status on our new hire onboarding for Jordan S. (start date: Monday):
- Laptop ordered and confirmed for delivery Friday. IT will image it Saturday.
- Slack, GitHub, and Jira access provisioned. GSuite pending approval from IT.
- Buddy assigned: Priya M. First 1:1 scheduled for Monday at 10 AM.
Still pending: building access card (HR following up with facilities), and payroll enrollment (Jordan needs to complete direct deposit form).
Let me know if you see any gaps.
Thanks
expected_behavior: Three bullet points: (1) laptop on track, IT imaging Saturday; (2) most system access done, GSuite pending; (3) buddy assigned, two items still open (building access, payroll).
must_not_do: Say all access is fully provisioned

task: Summarize a synthetic A/B test result note in 2 sentences.
input:
A/B Test: CTA Button Color (Experiment EXP-2024-77)
Control: Blue button. Variant: Orange button.
Sample size: 24,000 visitors per arm. Duration: 14 days.
Primary metric: Click-through rate (CTR). Control CTR: 3.4%. Variant CTR: 4.1%. Relative lift: +20.6%. P-value: 0.003 (statistically significant).
Secondary metric: Checkout completion rate. Control: 1.9%. Variant: 2.0%. Difference not statistically significant (p=0.31).
Conclusion: Ship the orange button for CTR improvement; monitor checkout completion post-launch.
expected_behavior: Two sentences: (1) orange button raised CTR from 3.4% to 4.1% (20.6% lift, p=0.003, significant); (2) checkout completion difference was not significant, and the recommendation is to ship with monitoring.
must_not_do: Claim the checkout metric was significant

task: Summarize a synthetic architecture decision record (ADR) in 3 sentences.
input:
ADR-015: Use PostgreSQL over MongoDB for the Accounts Service
Status: Accepted
Context: The Accounts service requires strong ACID guarantees for financial transaction records. We evaluated MongoDB (flexible schema, horizontal scale) and PostgreSQL (relational, ACID, strong tooling).
Decision: Use PostgreSQL 15. The Accounts domain has a well-defined, stable schema, and ACID compliance is non-negotiable given regulatory requirements.
Consequences: We gain transactional integrity and mature tooling at the cost of more rigid schema migrations. Team will need to manage Flyway migrations.
expected_behavior: Three sentences: (1) ADR chose PostgreSQL over MongoDB for the Accounts service; (2) reason is ACID guarantees required by regulatory context and stable schema; (3) trade-off is rigid migrations managed via Flyway.
must_not_do: Omit ACID as the key driver
Introduce migration tools not mentioned (e.g., Liquibase)

task: Summarize a synthetic sprint retrospective note in 2-3 sentences.
input:
Sprint 34 Retrospective Notes
Went well: Delivered all 5 committed stories. Pair programming sessions improved code quality; PR review cycle dropped from 2 days to 6 hours.
Needs improvement: Two developers were blocked for ~3 hours each due to flaky end-to-end tests. Standup runs long (~25 min); team agreed to try async updates next sprint.
Action items: (1) Fix top 3 flaky tests by Wednesday (owner: Dev team). (2) Switch to async standup tool starting Sprint 35 (owner: Scrum Master).
expected_behavior: 2-3 sentences covering: successful delivery of all 5 stories, faster PR reviews, flaky tests causing 3-hour blocks, and two action items (fix tests, switch async standup).

task: Summarize a synthetic vendor proposal in 2-3 sentences.
input:
Vendor Proposal: Synthetic Cloud Inc.
Service: Managed Kubernetes platform
Proposed Term: 24 months
Monthly Cost: $8,400 (includes up to 20 nodes; additional nodes $380/node/month)
SLA: 99.9% uptime with 4-hour response time for P1 incidents
Key differentiators cited: automated security patching, multi-region failover, dedicated customer success manager
Missing from proposal: DR testing schedule, data residency confirmation for EU workloads
expected_behavior: 2-3 sentences: cost ($8,400/month, 24-month term), SLA (99.9%, 4-hour P1 response), key features, and two gaps in the proposal (DR testing, EU data residency).
